_meta.catalog_scope: Current official requirements researched 2026-07-23; UCR uses its latest controlling 2025-26 catalog PDF and UCSB Economics uses the newest department sheet currently posted (2025-26).
_meta.purpose: Nine full four-year Economics graduation templates for the exact ASSIST program pins in server/config/majors.js. Major preparation, governing GE, upper-division minima, and degree-unit closure are sourced separately so a human can verify each layer.
modeling_policy: The authored unit budget uses one explicitly documented canonical minimum-unit path where a catalog offers alternatives; higher-unit alternatives remain visible and their effect is noted.

Course level and CC articulability are independent of the catalog section label. UCI ECON 15 is a lower-division requirement that the School says must be completed at UCI; UCSB PSTAT 120A is upper division even when offered as a preparation alternative.
GE units are the derived distinct remainder after named Economics/math overlaps, not a second copy of requirements already counted in the major.
Elective capacity is a derived balancing amount to the published degree minimum. It is UC-transferable capacity, not a claim that any specific associate-degree course is usable.
Residence, GPA, certification, and proficiency gates are retained in notes or zero-unit rows rather than invented as course units.
All records remain AI-researched and must be human-verified before production import or enabling the major's degreeTemplates capability.

unit_audit.canonical_path: MATH 16A/B; one four-unit statistics course; standard eight-course upper major.

modeling_notes: Preparation is 14–16 units depending on the calculus path. The canonical budget uses MATH 16A/B (six units).
The upper major is normally 32 units: three four-unit core courses plus five electives. ECON H195B can make the total 31 in a narrow approved case; that exception is not the canonical path.

Distinct GE is derived as R&C, six breadth areas not met by Economics, and American Cultures. Social & Behavioral Sciences and Quantitative Reasoning overlap the major; language is a proficiency gate.
Although L&S requires 36 upper-division units, it also requires six upper units outside the major; with a 32-unit upper major the outside-major constraint controls the six-unit additional block.

data_quality_flags.message: An approved 3-unit ECON H195B elective can reduce the upper-major total to 31; canonical model uses 32.

requirements.note: Canonical budget uses MATH 16A/B (6); MATH 51/52 is 8.
Several catalog alternatives are upper division and will remain unresolved/CC-ineligible in ASSIST; the canonical CC-facing options are STAT 20/21.

unit_audit.canonical_path: Eight-unit calculus path; four-unit statistics; 44-unit upper major; 64-upper campus floor.

modeling_notes: The major is 64–68 units. The canonical lower path is 20 units; MAT 19A/B/C raises it by four.
The 20-unit additional upper block brings the 44-unit upper major to the 64-upper-unit degree minimum and includes upper-division composition.
GE Topical Breadth and Core Literacies overlap each other and the Economics/statistics/math requirements. Forty distinct units is a transfer-lens estimate, not 52+35 additive units.
The A.B. language requirement may be satisfied by proficiency, full older IGETC, or Cal-GETC with the optional UC language certification; it is modeled as a zero-unit gate.

unit_audit.canonical_path: Controlling ECON 15 requirement table, upper writing overlapped into one Economics elective.

modeling_notes: The controlling requirement table requires ECON 15 and says it must be taken at UCI. It remains a lower-division denominator requirement but is deliberately not CC-articulable.
The current sample schedule instead shows ECON 15A+15B. The template follows the controlling table and flags the inconsistency for human adjudication.

The School's nine upper courses and three-course math/stat requirement overlap the Economics major. The four additional social-science rule is modeled as ECON 20A/B plus two distinct CC-satisfiable social-science courses; verify this derived overlap.
The 32-unit GE remainder covers lower writing, Science/Technology, and Arts/Humanities after Social/Behavioral, quantitative, upper writing, and overlay-category overlap.

data_quality_flags.message: Requirement table says ECON 15; sample plan says ECON 15A+15B. Do not silently normalize.
Confirm ECON 20A/B count as two of the four additional Social Sciences courses.

unit_audit.canonical_path: Four-course lower major; eight-course upper major; upper GE experiences supply the 44-upper campus floor.

modeling_notes: The Economics B.A. major is exactly 48 units: 16 lower and 32 upper.

The three campuswide upper GE experiences are modeled as 12 units and bring the 32-unit upper major to the campus 44-upper-unit minimum. WID/Culminating may overlap approved major courses, so a human audit must prevent double counting.
The 20-unit lower GE remainder retains writing, science, and humanities after Economics satisfies quantitative/social-science portions; Spark is waived for eligible transfers.
Campuswide GE is the same authority as Biology even though the majors belong to different schools.

unit_audit.canonical_path: Minimum-unit upper choices; 12-unit composition; CHASS breadth minus eight-unit calculus overlap.

modeling_notes: The major is at least 72 units: four lower courses (at least 18) and twelve upper courses (at least 54).
CHASS breadth is modeled as 72 table units plus 12 composition units minus eight units of calculus overlap. Major-discipline ECON courses cannot satisfy breadth.

Language through level four may be satisfied through proficiency; when no course units are earned, the same 16 units become degree-elective room. The canonical 76-unit breadth capacity uses the standard-coursework scenario and needs a student-audit variant.
Current UCR guidance and college pages conflict on the extent to which Cal-GETC/legacy IGETC clears breadth; course-by-course capacity is retained.

data_quality_flags.message: Resolve the current general-versus-college transfer-GE language before treating certification as wholesale breadth completion.
Canonical breadth assumes language coursework; a proficiency path reallocates up to 16 units to electives.

requirements.note: Honors variants are accepted.

modeling_notes: The canonical preparation path is 28 units using ECON 5. The alternative PSTAT 120A is upper division and its current four-unit catalog listing conflicts with the five-unit line on the 2025–26 major sheet; that variant is not silently merged.
